mapping.a_element: the family's limiting/quasi-extremal boundary separating regular, integrable-singularity, and Kerr regimes
mapping.b_element: the sharp exponent threshold s_c separating well-posedness from ill-posedness

brief_justification: The only overlap is a superficial 'boundary between regimes' theme and the coincidental reuse of the symbol 'a' (rotation/spin parameter in A versus the dispersion power exponent in B); the actual machinery differs entirely — A constructs axisymmetric black-hole metrics with a Kerr event horizon via a geometric interpolating ansatz, whereas B derives a critical Sobolev-type decay exponent for four-wave kinetic equations from trilinear collision-operator bounds and resonant-manifold geometry, so no role-for-role mapping of governing equations holds.
